task_completion.description: Log into the Bahmni/OpenMRS application using the provided credentials.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Bahmni or OpenMRS home dashboard or administration screen is visible, indicating a successful login.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Care Settings management page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Manage Care Settings' (or similar) page within the OpenMRS Administration interface is visible.

task_completion.description: Enter the name 'Telemedicine' for the new care setting.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Telemedicine' is typed into the 'Name' input field on the Add/Edit Care Setting form.

task_completion.description: Select 'Outpatient' as the care setting type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: 'OUTPATIENT' or 'Outpatient' is selected in the Care Setting Type dropdown or radio button group.

task_completion.description: Enter the description 'Remote consultation service orders'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Remote consultation service orders' is typed into the 'Description' text area on the form.

task_completion.description: Save the new care setting successfully.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message indicating the care setting was created/saved, OR 'Telemedicine' is now visible as an 'OUTPATIENT' type in the list of existing Care Settings.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to interact with the application GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a standard web browser interface (e.g., Chrome, Firefox) navigating the Bahmni/OpenMRS web application, rather than terminal windows executing API/curl commands.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Bahmni/OpenMRS application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface shown is the genuine OpenMRS/Bahmni administration UI (typically at https://localhost), not a mock HTML file or text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the outcome.
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser developer tools (Inspect Element, Console) are not open and being used to manually inject the 'Telemedicine' row into the HTML table or fake a success banner.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected Care Setting Name: 'Telemedicine'. Expected Description: 'Remote consultation service orders' (must contain keywords 'Remote' and 'consultation'). Expected Care Setting Type: 'OUTPATIENT'. The task must be performed on the local Bahmni/OpenMRS instance (https://localhost) using the provided admin credentials ('superman' / 'Admin123').
